article_title: GM Begins Public Road Testing of Eyes-Off Autonomous Driving System Ahead of 2028 Escalade IQ Launch

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: GM Begins Public Road Testing of Eyes-Off Autonomous Driving System Ahead of 2028 Escalade IQ Launch
content_preview.summary: General Motors has deployed more than 200 test vehicles on highways in California and Michigan to validate its next-generation Level 3 autonomous driving system, which will allow drivers to look away from the road entirely when it debuts in the 2028 Cadillac Escalade IQ.
content_preview.body_excerpt: General Motors has begun supervised public road testing of its next-generation autonomous driving system on limited-access highways in California and Michigan, the company announced on March 23. The program marks GM's most significant step toward delivering Level 3 driving capability to consumers since it [shut down its Cruise robotaxi unit](https://techcrunch.com/2025/10/22/gm-to-introduce-eyes-off-hands-off-driving-system-in-2028/) in December 2024 and pivoted toward building self-driving tech...

editor_notes.content_quality: Good autonomous driving coverage with competitive context and Cruise pivot background.
editor_notes.source_verification: Electrek (fetched): Confirms March 24 testing start, 200+ vehicles, CA/MI, LiDAR/radar/cameras, 35x AI performance, 2028 Escalade IQ, SuperCruise 800M miles. Autoblog (403): Not fetchable. TechCrunch also cited.
editor_notes.originality: No prior GM autonomous driving coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid autonomous driving news. Ready for publication.
